title: lithocarpus benzoin
intro: Lithocarpus benzoin, also known as the Ben Nut tree, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The tree is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The Ben Nut tree is used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve overall health. Lithocarpus benzoin can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to promote health and well-being.
